      Failed to load binary file
      Add project archive for submission to GitHub repositories
      Website

      A translation tool web app called "Polyglot". It lets users type or paste text into a source text box, choose a source and target language from dropdowns (English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Arabic, Hindi, Dutch, Swedish, Polish, Turkish, Vietnamese, Thai, Indonesian, Greek, Hebrew, Bengali, Urdu, Persian), and click Translate to get the translated text displayed in an output pane. Includes a swap button to flip source/target languages, a character counter (max 1000 chars), a copy-to-clipboard button for the translated output, and text-to-speech "Listen" buttons for both the source and translated text using the browser's built-in speech synthesis API. Translation should be powered by a free translation API call (e.g. MyMemory translation API: https://api.mymemory.translated.net/get?q=TEXT&langpair=SOURCE|TARGET) made from the backend. Design should have a warm, editorial, paper-like aesthetic: cream background, serif typography, a terracotta/rust accent color, hairline rules, minimal flat UI, no heavy gradients or rounded card shadows.

      27 minutes ago
      PolyglotWebsite

      give a folder or downloaded version with ull package so that i can submit on github repository

      7 minutes ago

      I'll package up the project as a zip file for you to download and push to GitHub.

      zip isn't installed — I'll use tar instead, which is always available.

      Now copying it into the workspace so you can download it directly.

      Download is ready! Here's what's included in the archive:

      • Full source code — frontend (artifacts/polyglot/), backend API (artifacts/api-server/), shared libraries (lib/)
      • No node_modules, no build artifacts, no .git history — just clean source

      To push to GitHub:

      # Extract the archive
      tar -xzf polyglot-project.tar.gz -C polyglot-project
       
      # Init and push
      cd polyglot-project
      git init
      git add .
      git commit -m "Initial commit: Polyglot translation app"
      git remote add origin https://github.com/your-username/polyglot.git
      git push -u origin main

      Then anyone cloning it just needs to run pnpm install to restore all dependencies.

      Everything is done — the archive has been cleaned up from the workspace. Let me know if you need anything else for the submission!

      Next, I can add a translation history panel that shows your recent translations with timestamps, so you can easily revisit or compare past results. Would you like me to do that?


      Make, test, iterate…
